things.name: Bom Jesus do Monte
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage baroque hilltop sanctuary reached via a spectacular monumental staircase of 577 steps or a funicular railway

things.name: Braga Cathedral (Sé de Braga)
things.description: Portugal's oldest cathedral founded in 1089 in Romanesque style, with the tomb of Henry of Burgundy and stunning baroque pipe organs

things.name: Theatro Circo
things.description: Magnificent belle époque theatre of 1915 fully restored and hosting world-class concerts, opera and international festivals

things.name: Museu dos Biscainhos
things.description: Elegant 18th-century baroque palace with Roman archaeological finds, period furniture and a formal garden with azulejo fountains

things.name: Estádio Municipal de Braga
things.description: One of Europe's most dramatic football stadiums, carved into a quarry face with only two stands and sheer rock behind the goal

things.name: Largo Carlos Amarante
things.description: Braga's liveliest square surrounded by baroque architecture and outdoor cafes, perfect for watching city life over a coffee

things.name: Museum of the Nogueira da Silva
things.description: Decorative arts museum in a neoclassical villa with silver, faience, paintings and furniture from a distinguished private collection

things.name: Sameiro Sanctuary
things.description: Neoclassical Marian sanctuary above Braga with sweeping views over the Minho valley and across to the Serra do Gerês mountains

things.name: Rua do Souto
things.description: Braga's main pedestrian shopping street lined with historic facades, jewellers and pastry shops celebrating the city's confectionery heritage

things.name: Guimarães Day Trip
things.description: UNESCO-listed historic city 25km from Braga, birthplace of the Portuguese nation, with its magnificent medieval castle
